title: per-game project manifest
description: Per-mod-project manifest. Lives at the working-directory root by convention as ./game.json (alongside ./orig, ./tables, ./names, ./lamps, ./sessions). Records which ROM this directory mods and the facts the tools need to drive it correctly without re-specifying them on every command — chiefly the emulation platform (wpc vs whitestar) and, for whitestar, the RAM address shadowing the ROM bank register. The setup skill writes a draft during project setup; you fill in the rest as you learn the game. replay.py reads 'platform'/'bank_shadow' to default --platform/--bank-shadow (an explicit CLI flag still wins). Unknown keys are allowed so the file can also serve as a human index of reference material (manual URL, ipdb id, notes).

rom.description: PinMAME ROM identifier / gamename (e.g. "lotr", "congo_21"). The basename used for ./names/<rom>.json, ./lamps/<rom>.json, and the staged ROM zip.

title.description: Human title (e.g. "The Lord of the Rings").

platform.description: Emulation/bank-resolution family. 'wpc' = Williams/Bally WPC (1990s). 'whitestar' = Sega/Stern Whitestar (e.g. LOTR). Selects ROM-bank resolution in the debugger/simulator; also a reminder that the disassembler (rom.py) auto-detects Whitestar geometry from ROM file sizes.

bank_shadow.description: Whitestar only: hex string for the game-RAM address mirroring the $3200 ROM-bank register (e.g. "0x0243", verified for LOTR). Used by replay.py/replay_host.py to resolve banked PCs in dbg/interactive. Ignored under platform 'wpc'.

table.description: Path (relative to the project root) to the .vpx table used for recording.

vbs.description: Path to the extracted table VBScript (e.g. "tables/<table>.vbs"). The atlas-building source of truth for switch/lamp wiring; extracted once during setup so other skills don't re-extract it.

ipdb.description: Internet Pinball Database machine id (ipdb.org/machine.cgi?id=...), for quick reference.

manual_url.description: URL of the operator manual used for the switch/lamp matrices (e.g. an archive.org djvu-text page).

examples.title: The Lord of the Rings
examples.table: tables/JP's Lord of the Rings (Stern 2003) v600.vpx
examples.vbs: tables/JP's Lord of the Rings (Stern 2003) v600.vbs
